content.decision: Landmark selection uses degree centrality (unique neighbour count) with a greedy diversity penalty that spreads landmarks across different tag clusters.
content.context: Initial weighted-degree approach produced identical scores for docs sharing common tags. All landmarks concentrated in the densest cluster. Reviewed Khuller et al. 'Landmarks in Graphs' paper for academic grounding.
content.why: Degree centrality identifies structural hubs with high blast radius. The diversity penalty (0.5 * covered_tags/total_tags per round) prevents all landmarks from clustering in one topic area, ensuring navigational coverage across the graph.
alternatives_considered: Weighted degree centrality — all docs with common tags scored identically
Pure degree centrality without diversity — landmarks concentrated in largest cluster
Metric dimension / resolving sets from Khuller paper — NP-complete, overkill for this use case
NetworkX or data science library — Go ecosystem lacks equivalent, algorithm is ~40 lines
impact: Top 2% of docs marked as landmarks with spread across tag clusters
centrality_score field normalized to [0,1] on all memory docs
Graph visualization uses landmark status for node highlighting
